content:
—————

We repositioned ourselves multiple times, trying to find an equation that yielded comforting results for both factors. The time spent in a hurry from one caress to the next, from one leg on top of the other, hands interlocked then broken then used a counter weight, from my side of the bed to her side to back again passed from moment to moment in flashes that rang bright in my ears and my eyes. When I cracked slits into my eyelids, I saw a face robbed of passion—a face that was gone and distracted; when she looked back she saw the same. She pushed herself from me and laid onto my side of the bed, which forced the moment into limbo. For the next ten minutes, we discussed whether or not whose pleasure informed the other. Can pleasure inform the other? Is there selfless pleasure? She mused


—————

out of what a lonely trumpet?

of voice a lonely trumpet. the vibration created by the body. from within the core of our physicality, from within the core of the subconscious can burst forth a sound that floats along with temporality through space. the voice is such a powerful tool, and since we have been using our respective voices since the cutting of the cord: we have grown to be able to control and manipulate in ways that an external instrument doesn’t allow. that is not to say that the limitations of a piano or a guitar are of any hinderance to creativity—it is to say however, that the voice can be like so many other instruments, be it one at a time or all sounding off together. the voice can have the sharp attack of a piano, or picked guitar, but it can also have the slow attack of a violin or some other electronic instrument. the voice is free to emote through vowels, the voice can be burdened by the nature of having to “say” something, but it does not necessarily have to be a burden—but a double edged weapon, one that feels something and says something all at once.
context: From document: Fiction creates distance from reality, allowing me to try and assume… — chunk 3 (chunk 3/4)
